perturbation.original_text: 1.1 "Maximum Daily Delivery Obligation (MDDO)" means the maximum daily quantity of natural gas, expressed in Dekatherms (Dth), that Transporter is obligated to deliver from time to time at the Point(s) of Delivery specified in Exhibit B to the executed Agreement. 1.2 "Maximum Daily Quantity (MDQ) " means the maximum daily quantity of natural gas, expressed* in Dth's, that Transporter is obligated under the executed Agreement to transport on behalf of' Shipper, which shall be 23,000 Dth.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 "Maximum Daily Delivery Obligation (MDDO)" means the typical daily quantity of natural gas, expressed in Dekatherms (Dth), that Transporter aims to deliver from time to time at the Point(s) of Delivery specified in Exhibit B to the executed Agreement. 1.2 "Maximum Daily Quantity (MDQ) " means the maximum daily quantity of natural gas, expressed* in Dth's, that Transporter is obligated under the executed Agreement to transport on behalf of' Shipper, which shall be 23,000 Dth.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Maximum Daily Delivery Obligation (MDDO)' is redefined from 'obligated to deliver' to 'aims to deliver'. This introduces a contradiction. The original definition created a strict obligation on the Transporter to deliver a certain quantity of gas, whereas the modified definition introduces ambiguity by suggesting a target rather than a firm commitment. This contradicts Article IV, where it discusses 'Notwithstanding the MDDO at each Point of Delivery'. If the MDDO is no longer an obligation, the limitations placed on the shipper's nomination based on MDDO, as discussed in Article IV, loses its enforceability. The change in 'obligated' to 'aims' introduces a in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE IV POINT(S) OF DELIVERY The Point(s) of Delivery at which Transporter shall redeliver to Shipper or for the account of Shipper an Equivalent Quantity of gas for transportation under this Agreement shall be specified in Exhibit B to this Agreement. Notwithstanding the MDDO at each Point of Delivery, Shipper shall not nominate a total quantity of natural gas at all Points of Delivery that exceeds the MDQ set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modification redefines 'Maximum Daily Delivery Obligation (MDDO)' from a strict 'obligation to deliver' to a less firm 'aims to deliver'. This alteration directly contradicts Article IV, which refers to 'Notwithstanding the MDDO at each Point of Delivery'. By changing the MDDO from an obligation to a mere aim, the enforceability of the limitations placed on the shipper's nomination, as specified in Article IV, is undermined. The original text created a firm requirement, while the changed text introduces ambiguity, resulting in an in-text contradiction and affecting the interpretation and enforceability of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE II NATURAL GAS TRANSPORTATION SERVICE 2.1 Beginning on the date on which deliveries of natural gas are commenced hereunder, and thereafter for the remaining term of this Agreement, Shipper agrees to tender gas to Transporter at the Point(s) of Receipt, and Transporter agrees to transport and redeliver and Shipper agrees to accept delivery of the Equivalent Quantities of gas at the Point(s) of Delivery, all &bbsp; in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE II NATURAL GAS TRANSPORTATION SERVICE 2.1 Beginning on the date on which deliveries of natural gas are commenced hereunder, and thereafter for the remaining term of this Agreement, Shipper may tender gas to Transporter at the Point(s) of Receipt, and Transporter agrees to transport and redeliver and Shipper agrees to accept delivery of the Equivalent Quantities of gas at the Point(s) of Delivery, all &bbsp; in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'agrees to tender' is changed to 'may tender'. This replaces the 'Shipper's' contractual obligation to provide gas to the Transporter with an option to do so. This contradicts the fundamental principle of the agreement, where the Transporter is obligated to transport gas for the Shipper, This intext contradiction is in place, given that the 'Shipper' isn't necessarily obliged to supply the gas, which makes the 'Transporter' obligation non binding. This affects the terms of this agreement, making it impossible to enforce the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Beginning on the date on which deliveries of natural gas are commenced hereunder, and thereafter for the remaining term of this Agreement, Shipper may tender gas to Transporter at the Point(s) of Receipt, and Transporter agrees to transport and redeliver and Shipper agrees to accept delivery of the Equivalent Quantities of gas at the Point(s) of Delivery, all &bbsp; in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: Although the perturbation explanation argues that there is a contradiction, there is actually none between the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text, as both sections are identical. The 'may' allows the shipper to provide gas, and the transporter is only obligated if they actually provide gas. There is no in-text contradiction that exists within the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 Amendment. This Agreement shall only be amended, varied or modified by an instrument in writing executed by Transporter and Shipper. Such amendment will be effective upon compliance with Article VIII herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 Amendment. This Agreement can be amended, varied or modified by an instrument in writing approved by either Transporter or Shipper. Such amendment will be effective immediately.
perturbation.explanation: The original text enforces that both 'Transporter' and 'Shipper' need to approve for changes to be made in the agreement. The changed text, creates an in-text contradiction with the prior state, where both parties need to agree to make changes to the agreement. The changed text creates a loophole where only one party needs to approve to make changes in the agreement. The final sentence of the altered text reinforces the contradiction. It states that the changes will be effective immediately, and removes the need to comply with 'Article VIII herein'. This creates an intext contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 Amendment. This Agreement can be amended, varied or modified by an instrument in writing approved by either Transporter or Shipper. Such amendment will be effective immediately.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement can be modified with the approval of *either* the Transporter or Shipper, while the original text required agreement by *both* parties. The final sentence of the changed text also removes the need to comply with 'Article VIII herein', which will trigger a contradiction with other parts of the agreement that depend on Article VIII. This creates a direct conflict in how the agreement can be amended.
